author: @danieljamesbindery
text: Me too, lmao.... iam an American, and I use metric for EVERYTHING. Ever since our crooked Poloticians sent all of our production overseas... they all use metric for their products. So now I use metrics for everything and our U.S. system slows me down on stuff🤣

author: @hyconiek22
text: I like your approach, I wonder how you can reconcile the use of a large surface on which the wind is to act while maintaining a high frequency of vibrations

author: @flemminghovkjrsrensen6523
text: You inspired me to get a 3D printer. I have a small Anycubic Kobra Go, and I love that little thing :-) It became even better, when I threw out Cura, and started using SuperSlicer. That really helped me a lot, because it's so beginner-friendly; even I can calibrate now, and get better prints :-)

author: @mikebond6328
text: I’m in the US and I use metric for everything I can because it’s easy and universal. I am one of the growing number of people who will be glad when it’s made standard everywhere.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it will make things a bit easier for sure lol

replies.author: @AAE-cg1il
replies.text: I do both systems……because it’s easy…….just numbers.

author: @VoidySA
text: If you struggle to get bed adhesion...a little hairspray will make your day !!! if you struggle to get the print off, wait till it cools down.
